name: the-boys-in-the-boat
description: Daniel James Brown's The Boys in the Boat — a team dynamics and perseverance toolkit chronicling the University of Washington's eight-oar crew team, nine working-class boys who overcame the Depression to win Olympic gold in front of Hitler, embodying teamwork, sacrifice, and collective flow.
useCase: Understand how nine individuals became one team — the power of synchronization, egoless teamwork, and the pursuit of 'swing' in any collective undertaking.
knownRisks: The book idealizes the working-class narrative — some historical details are simplified for narrative flow
The Nazi Germany context is secondary to the rowing story — not a comprehensive history of the 1936 Olympics
Rowing technique described may not be accessible to non-rowers without some explanation
